markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Detect PDFs with missing or weak text extraction and create repairable metadata instead of silent partial indexing. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: PdfTextLayerDetector leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: PdfTextLayerDetector is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 c1785c1a; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: FALSE-SUCCESS PROBE (the prompt's core MT-086 concern): has_text_layer() = has_text_operators && extracted_chars > 0 && error.is_none(). A scanned/image-only PDF that ALSO carries a tiny text operator — e.g. a 1-character watermark, a page number, or an INVISIBLE text layer (PDF text rendering mode Tr 3) emitting a single non-whitespace glyph — would set extracted_chars>=1 and be classified PartialTextLayer/TextLayer, then extracted as 'success' for that page, SILENTLY under-indexing the visual scan. The detector does NOT inspect text rendering mode (Tr 3 = invisible) and has NO minimum-char or char-density threshold. Real-world impact is low (most scans have zero text ops) but this is a genuine misclassification path that violates the 'never silent partial' spirit for the watermarked-scan case. HARDENING: treat Tr 3 / invisible text as non-text, and/or require a per-page minimum extracted_chars (e.g. > N) before calling a page text-bearing.

lopdf INTERNAL PANIC robustness: load_document/Content::decode/extract_text/get_page_resources are all Result-handled (no unwrap/expect on input), and garbage bytes yield a typed ParseError (test garbage_bytes_fail_typed_parse_error). BUT lopdf has a history of internal panics on crafted/malformed PDFs, and NONE of these calls is wrapped in std::panic::catch_unwind. A crafted PDF that panics inside lopdf would propagate up through run_extraction -> ingest_file_from_disk (called with ?) and ABORT THE WHOLE ingestion pass (one poison file kills the batch). HARDENING: wrap the lopdf entry points in catch_unwind and convert a caught panic into a typed Internal/ParseError receipt so one bad PDF degrades to a single failed file, not a dead pass.
evasion_probes: image-only PDF -> NoTextLayer verdict, image_only_pages counted, OCR_NEEDED hint, NEVER empty-success CONFIRMED (test image_only_pdf_yields_typed_no_text_layer_never_empty_success).
blank page (no text, no image) -> NoTextLayer, image_only_pages=0 CONFIRMED.
inline images (BI/ID/EI) counted as image content CONFIRMED.
encrypted PDF -> typed Encrypted error (is_encrypted check) CONFIRMED.
PDF with 0 pages -> typed ParseError CONFIRMED.
garbage/truncated bytes -> typed ParseError, no panic on the tested inputs CONFIRMED (but see catch_unwind gap above for crafted panic inputs).
evidence_checked: engine.rs:1139-1201 PDF extraction branch + secret preflight over extracted text

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG negative test green (8 hardening tests + 70 unit, no regression).

evidence: Hardened 5876feaf: ingestion secret-leak/lopdf/dead-letter fixes verified; boundary-seam fixture shows ZERO raw secret bytes in spans; DB redaction guard (0210) + receipt NOT NULL (0211) enforced.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-086 requires detecting PDFs with missing/weak text layers and producing repairable metadata plus receipts (against PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority) instead of silently indexing partial content. The code delivers this: a real pure-Rust lopdf-based per-page text-layer detector wired into the real ingestion engine, proven against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with image-only PDFs yielding typed NoTextLayer failures plus durable repair-queue rows, never empty-success.
findings: pdf.rs:52-56 has_text_layer() now requires has_text_operators AND extracted_chars>=MIN_TEXT_LAYER_CHARS(3) AND error.is_none() — fixes the prior adversarial 'false-success watermark/tiny-text' concern recorded in MT-086.lifecycle.adversarial_code_review.

pdf.rs:251-272 tracks PDF text render mode and excludes invisible text (Tr 3) from counting as a visible text layer — directly closes the OCR-overlay/hidden-text misclassification path. Proven by test invisible_text_pdf_is_not_a_text_layer (pdf.rs:718-735) and tiny_text_below_minimum_is_not_a_text_layer (pdf.rs:737-744).

pdf.rs:129-150 guard_lopdf wraps lopdf entry points in catch_unwind, converting a parser panic into a typed Internal error so one poison PDF degrades to one failed file rather than aborting the batch — fixes the second prior adversarial concern. Proven by guard_lopdf_converts_panic_to_typed_internal_error (pdf.rs:703-716) and truncated_pdf_header_fails_typed_not_panic (pdf.rs:692-701).

pdf.rs:348-420 extract_pdf_text returns a typed NoTextLayer error (never empty success) for image-only PDFs, with OCR_NEEDED guidance in detail_json (pdf.rs:85-98), and populates explicit failed_pages for partial extraction — matches MT requirement 'repairable metadata instead of silent partial indexing'.

engine.rs:1172-1234 wires the detector into the real ingestion path: success/partial/failed/blocked ExtractionStatus, spans per PDF page, secret preflight over extracted text, and error_detail carrying the repair report — the durable receipt-writing surface.

tests/knowledge_ingestion_pipeline_tests.rs:240-293 mt086_image_only_pdf_yields_typed_no_text_layer_and_repair_entry runs against real PG (ingestion_pg) and asserts receipt.status=Failed, error_class=NoTextLayer, empty spans, OCR_NEEDED in detail, and a durable repair-queue row with reason_class=NO_TEXT_LAYER linked to the receipt_id; mt230_weak_pdf...durable (line 334+) directly queries knowledge_ingestion_repair_queue and knowledge_ingestion_receipts tables.

knowledge_pg_support.rs:21 and knowledge_ingestion_support.rs:4-5 confirm the proof harness uses real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with the full migration chain and explicitly NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback — satisfies sub_rule_2 durable-authority touch.

Dependency added is lopdf 0.41 (MIT, pure Rust, no native deps, no daemon, default-features disabled); no forbidden SQLite/Docker/external search-engine/CDN-worker introduced — within packet allowed boundary.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork only): MT-086.lifecycle.hardening_closed.commit cites 5876feaf but the actual hardening landed in the worktree as c42e0e1d; stale commit pointer does not affect code correctness.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
